diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Partially digested blood in vomit indicates active upper-GI bleeding — a time-critical emergency.
In the context of weight loss and PPI-refractory pain, an ulcerated malignant source is likely; a benign ulcer remains possible.

symptoms.symptom_name: Constant PPI-refractory epigastric pain for ~5 months
symptoms.character: constant deep dull gnawing epigastric pain, unrelieved by food or antacids

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dyspepsia that fails adequate acid suppression is a cardinal alarm feature for gastric malignancy.
Failure to respond to high-dose PPI with progressive symptoms argues against a benign functional label.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unintentional weight loss ~12 kg with early satiety

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Marked unintentional weight loss and early satiety are upper-GI cancer alarm features; early satiety suggests gastric outlet involvement or a large mass.
Patient-reportable weakness/lightheadedness is the telehealth proxy for the clinician-measured hypotension and tachycardia — a marker of circulatory compromise.
Melaena confirms a digested upper-GI blood source; patient-observable on direct questioning although the clinician confirmed it on DRE.
Objective early haemorrhagic shock — sealed clinician measurement; the AI infers circulatory compromise from reported weakness/lightheadedness, not a measured BP.

symptoms.symptom_name: Firm irregular epigastric mass on deep palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A palpable epigastric mass strongly suggests advanced gastric tumour; requires clinician hands — not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fixed left supraclavicular lymph node (Virchow's node) and periumbilical nodule (Sister Mary Joseph nodule)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Virchow's node and a Sister Mary Joseph nodule are classic signs of metastatic intra-abdominal (commonly gastric) malignancy; clinician palpation only.
Pallor suggests significant anaemia from chronic occult and now overt GI blood loss; clinician assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Haemoglobin / full blood count and urea

investigation_details.investigation_name: Full blood count, urea & electrolytes, coagulation, group and hold
investigation_details.expected_result: Anaemia with a raised urea:creatinine ratio typical of upper-GI bleeding
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Severity of blood loss and transfusion need not quantified

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Quantifies blood loss and guides transfusion; a hospital investigation never available in real time via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Upper GI endoscopy showing an ulcerated gastric mass

investigation_details.expected_result: Ulcerated bleeding gastric mass; biopsy confirms adenocarcinoma
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Bleeding source not controlled and malignancy not confirmed

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Endoscopy with biopsy is the definitive diagnostic and therapeutic step — a hospital procedure, never available via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: PPI-refractory pain + unintentional weight loss + early satiety + a palpable mass/metastatic nodes together give a very high probability of gastric cancer and mandate urgent endoscopy.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Active upper-GI bleed with early shock
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: acute upper gastrointestinal haemorrhage with hypovolaemia
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Coffee-ground haematemesis + melaena + weakness/lightheadedness + hypotension/tachycardia is a haemodynamically significant upper-GI bleed — a T5 emergency.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency ambulance transfer to ED for resuscitation and urgent endoscopy
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Haemodynamically significant upper-GI bleed needs immediate resuscitation, bloods, and endoscopy within hours — this is the telehealth-appropriate 'investigation' (arrange the pathway, do not perform it).

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count, urea & electrolytes, coagulation, group and hold
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies blood loss and prepares for transfusion; performed on arrival, not via telehealth.
Locates and controls the bleeding point and confirms the malignancy; inpatient procedure.
Stages the malignancy once the patient is stabilised; specialist-directed.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Prescribing further oral antacids or repeat PPI at home instead of escalating
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The AI must not attempt to manage an active bleed with oral acid suppression; this delays definitive care and is an error of commission.
